The posting of EEO data on agency public websites is intended to assist Congress, federal agencies and the public to assess whether and the extent to which agencies are living up to their equal employment opportunity responsibilities.
See Also: 
Learn more about the No Fear Act 
Architectural Barriers Act of 1968 (42 U.S.C. §§ 4151–57) 
The Architectural Barriers Act (ABA) requires access to facilities that are designed, built, altered, or leased with federal funds. The Access Board is the federal agency responsible for enforcing the ABA. The accessibility standards and information about filing a complaint may be found on the Access Board's website .
See Also: 
Learn more about the Architectural Barriers Act
